---
title: "4.9 Enhanced For Loops"
subtitle: "ArrayList Traversals"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Enhanced For Loop

### Traverse every item

You can use an enhanced `for` loop to traverse all of the items in an `ArrayList`, just like with an array.

Use it when you only care about the values in the list and not their indices.

You cannot use the enhanced `for` loop if you want to add or remove elements while traversing an `ArrayList`.

## Concurrent Modification

### Do not change size during enhanced for

If the size of an `ArrayList` is modified by calling `add` or `remove` while it is being looped over, the loop will throw a `ConcurrentModificationException`.

If you need to modify an `ArrayList` while looping over it, use a regular `while` or `for` loop.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: listForEachLoop`

What does the following code do?

Guess before you run it.

Then add another enhanced for-each loop that computes the product of all the elements in `myList` by multiplying them.

Print out the product after the new loop.

## listForEachLoop Starter

```java
import java.util.*; // import all classes in this package.

public class Test1
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        ArrayList<Integer> myList = new ArrayList<Integer>();
        myList.add(50);
        myList.add(30);
        myList.add(20);
        int total = 0;
        for (Integer value : myList)
        {
            total += value;
        }
        System.out.println("Sum of all elements: " + total);

        // Write a for-each loop that computes the product
        // of all the elements in myList and print out the product.

    }
}
```

## listForEachLoop Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The output should contain:

```text
100
30000
```

The code should contain at least `2` enhanced for loops over `Integer` values.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: list_1`

The method `getScore` calculates and returns the score for a word game.

It loops through all elements in `wordList`.

::: {.tight-list}
- length `3`: add `1`
- length `4`: add `2`
- length greater than `4`: add `3`
:::

## list_1 Blocks

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
// Block A
public static int getScore(ArrayList<String> wordList)
{

// Block B
int score = 0;
for (String word : wordList)
{

// Block C
if (word.length() == 3)

// Block D
{
    score++;
}

// Block E
else if (word.length() == 4)
{
    score = score + 2;
}

// Block F
else if (word.length() > 4)
{
    score = score + 3;
}

// Block G
} // end for

// Block H
return score;
} // end method

// Distractor
if (word.length == 3)
```
:::

## Correct Order

### Score all words

```java
public static int getScore(ArrayList<String> wordList)
{
    int score = 0;
    for (String word : wordList)
    {
        if (word.length() == 3)
        {
            score++;
        }
        else if (word.length() == 4)
        {
            score = score + 2;
        }
        else if (word.length() > 4)
        {
            score = score + 3;
        }
    } // end for
    return score;
} // end method
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- use enhanced `for` when every value should be visited
- avoid adding or removing inside an enhanced `for`
- explain `ConcurrentModificationException`
- compute sum/product by accumulating inside the enhanced `for`
- use `for (Integer value : myList)` syntax for `ArrayList<Integer>`
- score every word in `wordList` before returning
:::

## End

### 4.9 Part 1 complete

Next: indexed and while traversals.
